title: NaN-unsafe sort in detection NMS panics and permanently poisons the ONNX session mutex
evidence: src-tauri/src/db_core/detection.rs:295 — `indices.sort_by(|&a, &b| confidences[b].partial_cmp(&confidences[a]).unwrap());` panics if any model confidence is NaN

src-tauri/src/db_core/detection.rs:160 — `let mut session = session_mutex.lock().unwrap();` on a std::sync::Mutex (detection.rs:6); nms() at :267 runs while this lock is held, so a panic poisons it and every subsequent detect() panics on the unwrap
src-tauri/src/commands/detection.rs:70 and :125 — detect() called from `detect_objects`/`detect_nsfw` Tauri commands; a panic there strands the invoke promise

Every other float sort in the codebase guards this: src-tauri/src/db_core/perceptual_hash.rs:81, src-tauri/src/db_core/db.rs:1878, src-tauri/src/db_core/color.rs:210 all use `unwrap_or(Ordering::Equal)` — detection.rs:295 is the lone outlier
AGENTS.md explicitly allows user-supplied local ONNX weights, which makes NaN outputs a realistic input
Runtime repro: standalone rustc program sorting `[NaN, 1.0, 0.5]` with the identical comparator panicked (`called Option::unwrap() on a None value`, exit 101)
proposed_fix: Replace with `confidences[b].total_cmp(&confidences[a])` (or `partial_cmp(...).unwrap_or(Ordering::Equal)` to match sibling sites), and optionally filter non-finite confidences before NMS.

title: Trash/permanent-delete swallow per-file failures end-to-end; UI can claim success while files remain on disk
evidence: src-tauri/src/commands/library.rs:135-137 — `trash_images` logs trash::delete failures with eprintln only and returns a success count; no error detail reaches the caller
src-tauri/src/commands/library.rs:119 and :125 — `let _ = state.db.mark_file_missing(...)` and `let _ = state.action_manager.record_action(...)` discard DB/undo-log errors after a successful trash
src-tauri/src/commands/library.rs:159-161 — `delete_images_permanently` uses `std::fs::remove_file(path).is_ok()` with no logging at all; failures are completely invisible
src/lib/menu.ts:366 — `await trashImages(ids);` ignores the returned count, then `reloadAfterImageRemoval(ids)` removes ALL requested ids from the UI even if the backend trashed only some
src/lib/components/ContextMenu.svelte:312 — multi-image trash also discards the count and has no try/catch, so an invoke rejection becomes an unhandled promise rejection
src/routes/+page.svelte:93-100 and :136-142 — `executeTrash`/`handlePermanentDelete` show no feedback at all when count is 0 (keystroke silently does nothing) and have no try/catch around the invoke
proposed_fix: Change trash_images/delete_images_permanently to return per-id results (or {succeeded, failed: [(id, error)]}); surface partial failures via showToast (the toast infra at src/lib/stores.ts:342 already supports detail text); only remove successfully-trashed ids from the images store; wrap all call sites in try/catch like menu.ts:365-371 already does.

title: Inconsistent Mutex strategy: std::sync::Mutex + lock().unwrap() in services while db.rs uses parking_lot, leaving a poisoning cascade class open
evidence: src-tauri/src/db_core/db.rs:8 — core DB deliberately uses `parking_lot::Mutex` (no poisoning)
src-tauri/src/services/jobs.rs:3,64 — JobRegistry uses std Mutex with ~15 `.lock().unwrap()` sites
src-tauri/src/services/undo.rs:5,81 — cursor lock, same pattern
src-tauri/src/db_core/embeddings.rs:6,299 — ONNX embedding session behind std Mutex `.lock().unwrap()`; same panic-while-locked exposure as CQ-1 (image decode + tensor fill run under the lock)
src-tauri/src/db_core/detection.rs:6,160 — the concrete poisoning trigger documented in CQ-1
proposed_fix: Migrate the remaining std::sync::Mutex uses in services/ and db_core/ to parking_lot::Mutex (already a dependency, Cargo.toml:56) so a single panic cannot convert every later lock into a panic; alternatively use lock().unwrap_or_else(PoisonError::into_inner).

title: Store subscription leaks in Grid and TabBar: manual .subscribe() during component init with no unsubscribe
evidence: src/lib/components/Grid.svelte:26 — `thumbnailSize.subscribe(v => size = v);` return value discarded
src/lib/components/Grid.svelte:29 — `gridGap.subscribe(v => gap = v);` same
src/lib/components/TabBar.svelte:12 — `thumbnailSize.subscribe(v => size = v);` same

Svelte only auto-unsubscribes `$store` syntax; manual subscribe() in component init leaks one subscription per mount, and Grid remounts on every view switch, keeping destroyed-component closures alive for the app lifetime
proposed_fix: Replace with the `$thumbnailSize`/`$gridGap` auto-subscription (or `$derived($thumbnailSize)`), or capture the unsubscriber and call it in onDestroy where local mutation is needed (TabBar's setSize).

title: Dead component: SessionTimeline.svelte is referenced nowhere in the codebase
evidence: src/lib/components/SessionTimeline.svelte — `grep -rn SessionTimeline src/ tests/` returns zero matches outside the file itself (exit 1)

Introduced in commit 45b60846b ('feat: add stateful Cull workflows') and never wired into any route or component; it still calls listSessionEvents from $lib/api, so it reads as live code to contributors of the public repo
proposed_fix: Either wire it into the sessions UI (SessionSwitcher) or delete the file; for an open-source launch, unreferenced components inflate the perceived API surface and confuse first-time contributors.

title: PromptResubmitDialog cost-estimate effect lacks the stale-response guard used everywhere else; stale estimate can overwrite a newer one before paid generation
evidence: src/lib/components/PromptResubmitDialog.svelte:50-57 — `$effect` fires estimateGenerationCost(provider, model, size, quality, n) and writes `costEstimate = c` in .then() with no sequence token; rapid param changes can resolve out of order, displaying the cost for the wrong n/quality on a dialog that submits paid API generations

The codebase's own convention proves the gap: src/lib/components/Loupe.svelte:164-176 uses `histogramRequestSeq` and EmbeddingExplorer.svelte:245-257 uses `selectedGenerationLoadSeq` to discard stale async results in the same situation
proposed_fix: Add the same seq-token guard: `const seq = ++costEstimateSeq;` before the call and `if (seq === costEstimateSeq) costEstimate = c;` in then/catch.

title: Import pipeline silently discards metadata/side-effect errors (batch links, session collection, lineage, session events)
evidence: src-tauri/src/commands/import.rs:94 — `let _ = db.set_image_batch(id, &batch);`
src-tauri/src/commands/import.rs:98 — `let _ = db.add_to_collection(active_session_id, &refs);` (imported images silently missing from the active session)
src-tauri/src/commands/import.rs:100-101 — lineage detection and session-event logging errors also discarded; duplicated at :186-193 for the second import path
src-tauri/src/commands/import.rs:648 and :692 — `let _ = state.db.store_detections(...)` drops detection persistence errors during import-time auto-detection
proposed_fix: At minimum route these through safe_eprintln with image ids (matching the style at commands/detection.rs:81); ideally count failures and include them in the import result so the UI banner can say 'imported N, M metadata steps failed'.

title: MCP export_images writes to arbitrary output_dir with no path confinement
evidence: src-tauri/src/services/export.rs:60-67 — output_dir taken verbatim from params and created with fs::create_dir_all, no home/temp confinement and no '..' rejection

src-tauri/src/mcp/tools.rs:2633-2692 — export_images scope-checks the SOURCE images but never the destination; reachable by any curator/operator/admin token (capability export:read) over the MCP HTTP server

src-tauri/src/commands/static_publishing.rs:816-867 — resolve_export_root shows the project's own confinement pattern (reject '..', canonicalize, require under $HOME or temp) that export_images does not apply
SECURITY.md:48-49 claims 'Export paths are validated against the user's home directory using the same rules as deep links' — true for static publishing, false for export_images
proposed_fix: Route export_images output_dir through the same confinement as resolve_export_root (or path_policy::validate_path): reject ParentDir components, canonicalize, require under $HOME/temp, and consider blocking hidden dirs (~/.ssh, ~/Library/LaunchAgents) for token-authenticated callers. Add unit tests mirroring static_publishing's. Damage is bounded (image-format content, sanitized filenames per export.rs:321-332) and HTTP MCP is off by default, hence pre-launch not blocker.

title: CSP connect-src whitelists three AI provider hosts the frontend never calls (ready-made exfiltration channel)
evidence: src-tauri/tauri.conf.json:27 — connect-src includes https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com, https://api.openai.com, https://openrouter.ai

All provider traffic is backend-side Rust reqwest: src-tauri/src/commands/embeddings.rs:11,885-903, src-tauri/src/db_core/gemini.rs:28; grep of src/ shows no fetch/XHR to these hosts (only UI labels in src/lib/components/McpSettings.svelte:46-48)
src-tauri/Cargo.toml:31-50 — no tauri-plugin-http, so these CSP entries serve no IPC purpose; any renderer XSS could POST library data or pasted-key input to openrouter.ai/api.openai.com unimpeded
proposed_fix: Remove the three external hosts from connect-src, leaving "'self' ipc: http://ipc.localhost". Run the E2E smoke suite + a manual embeddings/validate-key flow to confirm nothing in the webview actually depended on them.

title: Developer-personal directory $HOME/.codex/generated_images baked into shipped asset-protocol scope
evidence: src-tauri/tauri.conf.json:41 — assetProtocol scope ships "$HOME/.codex/generated_images/**/*" to every user install
SECURITY.md:50-56 documents it as one of only three asset-scope directories, so the posture doc canonizes a personal workflow path
Unlike $APPDATA/thumbnails and $APPDATA/generated, this grants the webview read access to a third-party tool's output directory that Cull does not own
proposed_fix: Remove the hardcoded .codex scope from tauri.conf.json (and SECURITY.md). If the codex-import integration is wanted, surface generated images via the existing import/thumbnail pipeline or a user-configured watched folder instead of a static webview-readable scope.

title: Audit-log gaps: UI-side token create/rotate/revoke and failed HTTP auth are not logged; tokens cannot expire
evidence: src-tauri/src/commands/mcp.rs:6-36 — create_mcp_token/revoke_mcp_token/rotate_mcp_token call services::tokens directly with no log_audit, while the same operations via MCP tools are logged through call_tool (src-tauri/src/mcp/tools.rs:2841-2877)
src-tauri/src/mcp/http.rs:352-377 — invalid/missing Bearer returns 401 with only safe_eprintln, no mcp_audit_log entry, so brute-force or leaked-token probing leaves no queryable trace
src-tauri/src/services/tokens.rs:171-209 — create_token always sets expires_at: None and exposes no expiry parameter, although validate_token honors expiry (tokens.rs:248-254)
proposed_fix: Add log_audit calls (token_id, 'create_token'/'rotate_token'/'revoke_token', status) to the Tauri commands; log auth failures with a synthetic tool_name like '_auth_failed'; add optional expires_at to create_token and the UI/MCP create paths.

title: Personal absolute paths in two tracked docs and throughout git history (no secrets found)
evidence: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-03-release-skill.md:497,503,550,641 and docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-30-clipboard-monitor-design.md:69 — /Users/glebkalinin/... paths in tracked files

History commits embed personal photo/project paths, e.g. dec06d40c adds "/Users/glebkalinin/Documents/Pictures/20210317 a53/Capture/20210317 a53 0028.RAF" to src-tauri/src/raw/fuji.rs; 8b7b00b32 adds /Users/glebkalinin/ai_projects/claude-code-lab/... sidecar outputs

Credential scan of full history (git log -p -G for AIza/sk-/ghp_/PRIVATE KEY patterns) surfaced only intentional test sentinels from security-fix commit 9b5698f43; working tree has no .env files and .gitignore excludes them
proposed_fix: Before the repo goes public, replace the hardcoded /Users/glebkalinin paths in the two tracked docs with $HOME or repo-relative placeholders (S, do it pre-launch since it is two files). Do NOT rewrite history for the path mentions — they reveal only a username and photo-folder names, and a rewrite would invalidate all clones/tags; record the decision in the release notes instead.

title: Default window capability grants opener:allow-open-path over all of $HOME plus process:default
evidence: src-tauri/capabilities/default.json:11-17 — opener:allow-open-path allowed for {"path": "$HOME/**/*"} and {"path": "$APPDATA/**/*"} on windows main and window-*
src-tauri/capabilities/default.json:23 — process:default (exit/restart) also granted to the renderer

Mitigating context: CSP is strict (tauri.conf.json:24-34) and preview-display window gets a narrower capability set (src-tauri/capabilities/preview-display.json), so exploitation requires renderer compromise first
proposed_fix: Tighten when convenient: 'Reveal in Finder' legitimately needs arbitrary library locations, but consider routing reveal/open through a Rust command that checks the path is a library-registered image/folder, then drop the blanket $HOME/**/* opener grant and process permission from the default capability.

title: GitHub Actions billing failure: CI and signed Release pipeline have never produced an artifact
evidence: gh run list --workflow=release.yml: v0.2.1 run 26952066452 and v0.2.0 run 26893830563 both 'completed failure' in ~5s
gh run view 26952066452: 'The job was not started because recent account payments have failed or your spending limit needs to be increased' (both macOS matrix jobs)
gh run list --workflow=ci.yml: latest main push run 27236486149 (2026-06-09) failed the same way — ubuntu and macos jobs never started
gh release list: empty — git tags v0.2.0 and v0.2.1 exist locally but no GitHub release or DMG exists

.github/workflows/release.yml:46-91 has correct Developer ID + notarization env wiring (APPLE_CERTIFICATE, APPLE_ID, APPLE_TEAM_ID, TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY) with non-empty secret assertions, so config is ready once runners start
proposed_fix: Fix GitHub billing/spending limit, then re-run the v0.2.1 Release workflow (workflow_dispatch or re-tag), confirm green CI on main, and verify the produced DMG with codesign --verify --deep --strict, spctl --assess, and xcrun stapler validate. Publish the GitHub release with notes from CHANGELOG.md (which is current to 0.2.1). The release definition (installable signed macOS app + public repo with green CI) is unreachable until this is fixed.

title: Copyright-year drift: uncommitted NOTICE edit, About dialog still says 2025-present
evidence: git status: NOTICE modified, uncommitted; git diff NOTICE: 'Copyright 2025-present' -> 'Copyright 2026-present'
src/lib/components/AboutDialog.svelte:125 hardcodes '(c) 2025-present Gleb Kalinin'
git log --reverse: first commit 9361d6129 dated 2026-05-07, so 2026 is the correct first year
src/lib/open-source-release-contract.test.ts:36-46 checks NOTICE content but not year alignment with the About dialog, so this drift passes the contract test (vitest run: 5/5 pass)
proposed_fix: Update AboutDialog.svelte:125 to '(c) 2026-present', commit it together with the pending NOTICE change, and extend open-source-release-contract.test.ts to assert the same copyright year string appears in both NOTICE and AboutDialog.svelte.

title: npm run audit:supply-chain cannot pass: required tooling absent, no deny.toml, not wired into CI
evidence: npm run audit:supply-chain exits 1: 'missing required tool: cargo-deny' (and cargo-audit was also missing until installed during this audit)

scripts/supply-chain-audit.sh:21-28 runs 'cargo deny check advisories licenses bans sources' but no deny.toml exists anywhere in the repo (find maxdepth 3: none), so even with cargo-deny installed the check has no committed policy
grep -i 'audit|supply' .github/workflows/ci.yml .github/workflows/release.yml: no matches — neither workflow runs any supply-chain audit

Manual results during this audit: npm audit = 0 vulnerabilities (prod and dev); cargo audit (v0.22.2, 678 crates) = 0 vulnerabilities, exit 0, 17 warnings — unmaintained gtk3-stack/unic crates and glib 0.18.5 unsoundness RUSTSEC-2024-0429, all Linux-only Tauri transitive deps
docs/OPEN_SOURCE_AUDIT.md:63-66 documents cargo-deny and CycloneDX SBOM as part of the release posture, so docs promise more than the repo can execute
proposed_fix: Commit a src-tauri/deny.toml encoding the license policy the docs claim, add a CI job (or release-gate step) that installs cargo-deny/cargo-audit and runs scripts/supply-chain-audit.sh check, and record the current cargo-audit warnings (Linux-only gtk3 stack) as accepted in deny.toml ignore list with rationale. Without this, the documented 'run before release' gate silently fails on any fresh machine.

title: Repo-going-public content review: tracked bd issue database, internal audit docs, and absolute personal paths ship publicly
evidence: .beads/issues.jsonl (238 lines) and .beads/interactions.jsonl (112 lines) are git-tracked (git ls-files) despite .gitignore:40 '.beads/'; they contain the full internal issue history including security-issue descriptions and owner email glebis@gmail.com on every record
docs/cull-audit-2026-06-03.md — a full external security/UX audit — is tracked and will ship with the public repo (presence noted only; not read per fresh-eyes rule)
docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-03-release-skill.md:497,503,550,641 and docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-30-clipboard-monitor-design.md:69 contain absolute personal paths (/Users/glebkalinin/...)
AGENTS.md:148-159 ships personal machine references (Obsidian vault ~/Brains/brain/, ~/.Codex/refs/* email/telegram rule files)

docs/ also carries internal working artifacts a stranger gains nothing from: 2026-05-10-vision-brainstorm-raw.md, dev-workflow-audit-2026-06-02.md, tooling-research-2026-06-03.md, settings-mockup-*.json, oss-strategy-explorer.html
proposed_fix: Make an explicit ship/trim decision before flipping the repo public: either accept the bd jsonl files as transparency (and scrub closed security-issue detail plus the per-record email) or stop tracking them; move personal-path-bearing superpowers plans/specs and raw brainstorm/internal-audit docs to a private archive or rewrite paths as $REPO-relative; trim AGENTS.md personal reference section to project-relevant entries.

title: Personal Codex workflow path ($HOME/.codex/generated_images) baked into every user's asset-protocol scope
evidence: src-tauri/tauri.conf.json:41 assetProtocol allow includes '$HOME/.codex/generated_images/**/*' alongside the two $APPDATA scopes
src/lib/view-utils.ts:126 hardcodes CODEX_GENERATED_IMAGES_SEGMENT = '/.codex/generated_images/'
SECURITY.md:48-52 documents this directory as one of only three asset-protocol scopes, presenting a maintainer-specific workflow as product security posture
src/lib/asset-protocol-config.test.ts:27 pins the path in a contract test, so it is deliberate, not accidental
proposed_fix: Decide whether the Codex generated-images integration is a product feature for strangers; if yes, document it in README/USER_GUIDE as a first-class integration; if not, remove it from the default asset scope (and the contract test) or make it an opt-in configurable library root, then update SECURITY.md. Shipping a hidden hardcoded scope for one external tool is confusing in a public security model.

title: README and SECURITY.md version staleness: status pinned to v0.1.0 / 0.1.x while shipping 0.2.1, and no install path for non-developers
evidence: README.md:30 '## Current Status (v0.1.0)' while package.json:3, src-tauri/Cargo.toml:3, and src-tauri/tauri.conf.json all say 0.2.1
SECURITY.md:55-59 Supported Versions table lists only '0.1.x | Yes'

README.md:222-229 Quick Start is developer-only (git clone + npm run tauri dev); there is no download/install section for a stranger receiving the soft launch, even though the release definition is an installable signed macOS app
README otherwise adequate for a stranger: clear pitch (lines 1-15), docs index (24-31), keyboard reference, pinned toolchain (227), license section (244-249)
proposed_fix: Bump the README status header to the released version (or drop the version from the heading), update the SECURITY.md supported-versions table to 0.2.x, and add a Download/Install section pointing at the signed DMG release asset once HYG-001 is fixed. Consider asserting README/SECURITY version freshness in the release skill's versionFiles gate (release.config.json).

title: Verified-clean baseline (informational): license alignment, license audit, lockfiles, npm/cargo vuln scan, migration upgrade path all pass
evidence: License identifier aligned everywhere: package.json:34 'Apache-2.0', src-tauri/Cargo.toml:6 license = 'Apache-2.0', package-lock.json:10, README.md:244-249, NOTICE line 4, src/lib/components/AboutDialog.svelte:114, full Apache-2.0 text in both LICENSE and LICENSE.md
npm run audit:licenses: 'license audit passed', exit 0 — 144 npm + 677 cargo packages, source matches 0, blockedBuiltInDownloads 0
Lockfiles committed: package-lock.json and src-tauri/Cargo.lock in git ls-files; release.config.json:9 also pins Cargo.lock in the release gate
npm audit and npm audit --omit=dev: 0 vulnerabilities; cargo audit: 0 vulnerabilities (warnings covered in HYG-003)

Upgrade path runtime-verified: cargo test db_core::db::migration_safety_tests = 7 passed, covering legacy->21, v20->v21 (test_open_accepts_schema_version_20_database), v21 re-open, future-version rejection, pre-migration backup, failed-step rollback; CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 21 at src-tauri/src/db_core/db.rs:14; compat fixture src-tauri/tests/fixtures/db/v21.db exists

SECURITY.md present with a concrete threat model; CHANGELOG.md current to 0.2.1 (2026-06-04); updater pubkey + endpoint configured at src-tauri/tauri.conf.json:57-62 with a rotated signing key per CHANGELOG 0.2.1
proposed_fix: No action required — record these as passed checks in the release report so Track A does not re-spend time on them.

title: Global Tab hijack makes every control unreachable by keyboard (no focus order at all)
evidence: src/lib/keys.ts:338-342 — `if (e.key === 'Tab' ...) { e.preventDefault(); cycleViewMode(...) }` runs for all non-editable targets, so Tab never moves focus
Runtime: with focus on BODY, pressing real Tab twice cycled Grid→Loupe→Compare while document.activeElement stayed BODY both times
src/app.css:85-87 defines a :focus-visible outline that keyboard users can never reach for buttons like '+ Import Folder' (Sidebar.svelte:736)
Spec scope explicitly includes 'focus order through import → grid → loupe → export' — there is no traversable focus order anywhere
proposed_fix: Move view-cycling off bare Tab (e.g. keep Ctrl+Tab / [ ] / existing ⌘1-7) or only intercept Tab when document.activeElement is body, restoring native Tab/Shift+Tab focus traversal through sidebar, grid, loupe and export controls.

title: Backend/init failure is silently rendered as a healthy empty library
evidence: src/routes/+page.svelte:359 — `init().catch(e => console.error('Failed to initialize app:', e))` with no UI surface
src/lib/components/Sidebar.svelte:34-43 — listFolders/listCollections failures are console.error only

Runtime: with all invokes failing (browser tab on :1420), the UI showed 'All Images (0)' and the normal 'No images loaded' empty state with zero error indication — identical to a genuinely empty library
A user with a corrupt/locked cull.db would conclude their library is gone and possibly re-import or churn
proposed_fix: On init/list failure, show an error state distinct from the empty state (banner or toast with retry + path to the DB), and gate the 'No images loaded' copy on a successful first query.

title: First-run sidebar leads with AI-model jargon and a dead-end 'Install model manually' instruction
evidence: Runtime (first-run, 0 images): sidebar prominently shows 'AI MODELS / YOLO manual install / nano 6MB…medium 50MB / Install model manually / NudeNet manual install / Ollama offline' before the user has imported anything
src/lib/components/Sidebar.svelte:551,562,571,576 — 'manual install' / 'Install model manually' have no link, no target path, no docs reference; nothing is clickable
src/lib/components/Sidebar.svelte:338 — `aiExpanded = $state(true)` so the section is expanded by default on first run
A stranger cannot know what YOLO/NudeNet are, why they'd want them, or where a 'manual install' goes
proposed_fix: Collapse AI MODELS by default until the library has images; replace the dead-end text with a help affordance (expected file path + link to docs explaining the manual-install policy), and soften 'offline' for Ollama to an optional-integration framing.

title: Empty-library state is not actionable and undersells import paths
evidence: src/lib/components/Grid.svelte:197-198 — 'No images loaded / Use the sidebar to import a folder' with no button
Runtime: the referenced '+ Import Folder' button (Sidebar.svelte:736-737) sits at the very bottom of the sidebar, ~700px below the section the eye scans first, below AI MODELS/CLIPBOARD MONITOR noise
Drag-and-drop import exists (+page.svelte:510-511 'Drop to import') but the empty state never mentions it
proposed_fix: Make the empty state the onboarding: an Import Folder button in the empty-state body plus 'or drop a folder anywhere' hint; consider moving Import Folder to the top of the sidebar.

title: Toasts carry all success/error feedback but have no aria-live/status role
evidence: src/lib/components/Toast.svelte — grep for aria-live/role returns nothing (exit 1), while Canvas/Grid/Export/Sidebar all set aria-live on their regions
Import success/failure (Sidebar.svelte:322-330), trash (+page.svelte:96), permanent delete (+page.svelte:138) and collection errors (ImportBanner.svelte) are reported exclusively via showToast
Screen-reader users get no notification of import results or destructive-action outcomes
proposed_fix: Add role="status" aria-live="polite" (and aria-live="assertive" for error type) to the toast container.

title: Search bar has two adjacent unlabeled '×' buttons with different behaviors (clear vs close)
evidence: Runtime aria scan of the open command bar: `[{label:'Switch dictation language'...},{label:'Toggle voice input'...},{label:null,txt:'×'},{label:null,txt:'×'}]` — two ×s with no aria-label and no title
src/lib/components/CommandBar.svelte:464 (.clear-btn '×') and :466 (.close-btn '×'); also :430 (.pill-close '×' on filter chips, unlabeled)
Visually identical side-by-side ×s force trial-and-error even for sighted users
proposed_fix: aria-label + title ('Clear query' / 'Close search' / 'Remove filter'), and visually differentiate clear (small, inside input) from close.

title: Shortcut help and the command palette are themselves undiscoverable (no '?' key, ⌘P advertised nowhere)
evidence: Runtime: dispatching '?' opens nothing; no shortcuts overlay appears
src/lib/keys.ts:301-311 — palette opens only on ⌘P/⌘⇧P; no 'k'-metaKey or '?' handler exists in keys.ts
src/lib/command-palette.ts:638 — `shortcutsOpen.set(true)` is reachable only as a palette command, so the shortcuts panel requires the palette the user doesn't know about
src/lib/components/StatusBar.svelte:79-94 — the hint strip lists 13 chord hints but never mentions ⌘P or help
proposed_fix: Bind '?' (and Shift+/) to shortcutsOpen, add '?:help' and '⌘P:commands' to the status-bar hints, and consider ⌘K as a palette alias.

title: Status-bar shortcut hints are static and wrong outside Grid
evidence: src/lib/components/StatusBar.svelte:79-94 — hints (space:select, 1-5:rate, hjkl:nav, b:batch…) render unconditionally for every mode

Runtime: identical hint strip shown in Export, Canvas, Embeddings views where several do not apply; in Loupe the NSFW overlay says 'hold Space to peek' (runtime screenshot) while the status bar simultaneously says 'space:select'
proposed_fix: Derive the hint list from viewMode (the mode label is already derived at StatusBar.svelte:40), showing only bindings active in the current view.

title: Loupe and Embeddings empty states name actions without offering them
evidence: src/lib/components/Loupe.svelte:541 — bare 'No image selected', no hint how to select (runtime screenshot: full black screen, sidebar hidden)

src/lib/components/EmbeddingExplorer.svelte:2102-2108 — 'Model Required / Download CLIP ViT-B/32…', 'API Key Required / Set an API key…' with no button; the actual affordance is an unlabeled gear in the MODEL panel
Runtime: Embeddings view with 0 images still renders the full Z PRESET/SPACE PRESET/Spacing/Z depth/Perspective slider stack — jargon-heavy dead controls for a first-run user
proposed_fix: Loupe: add 'Press ⌘1 to pick an image in Grid'. Embeddings: put a Download/Settings button inside the empty state and disable the projection controls until points exist.

title: Raw classifier class names leak into the loupe info panel
evidence: Runtime loupe info panel (mock data): 'DETECTIONS / OBJECTS person 0.93 / NSFW EXPOSED_BREAST_F 0.88' — raw snake_case NudeNet label truncated mid-token
Terminology elsewhere is humanized (Accept/Reject per context-menu-labels.test.ts), so raw EXPOSED_BREAST_F is incoherent with the rest of the UI
proposed_fix: Map detector class IDs to human labels (and title-case object classes) at display time in the loupe detections panel.

title: Boilerplate document title 'Tauri + SvelteKit + Typescript App'
evidence: src/app.html:7 — `<title>Tauri + SvelteKit + Typescript App</title>`; runtime-confirmed via document.title on :1420
Production window title is fine (src-tauri/tauri.conf.json:15 'Cull'), but the HTML title is what assistive tech announces as document title and what shows in any web/dev context of a public repo
proposed_fix: Set the title to 'Cull' in src/app.html.

title: Minor Tokyo Night token drift (off-palette red, raw #fff)
evidence: src/lib/components/Thumbnail.svelte:305 — `color: #f87171` (Tailwind red-400) instead of --red #f7768e
src/lib/components/Loupe.svelte:888,934 — `color: #fff` instead of --text

Canvas-drawn hexes (EmbeddingExplorer, ContactSheetDialog) are justifiable since ctx can't read CSS vars, and they do use Tokyo Night values; runtime contrast scan across empty + populated grid found zero AA failures on real text (only decorative glyphs ☰/| and an emoji pin button)
proposed_fix: Replace the two literal colors with var(--red)/var(--text); optionally read tokens via getComputedStyle for canvas contexts.

title: Import error detail is summarized to a count with no way to see which files failed
evidence: src/lib/components/Sidebar.svelte:319-321 — `lastResult += ', N errors'` appended to an 8-second toast; result.errors contents are never shown anywhere
Otherwise the import flow is good (progress on the button Sidebar.svelte:737, success/error toasts, post-import 'Save Import as Collection' banner in ImportBanner.svelte)
proposed_fix: Make the errors count clickable (or log to a viewable diagnostics panel) listing failed paths and reasons.

title: Embedding job throughput: sequential single-threaded pipeline decodes full-res originals for 224px CLIP input
evidence: src-tauri/src/services/model_pipeline.rs:142-257 — strict per-image loop: DB lookup, decode, inference, 2 DB writes per image, no batching or parallelism
src-tauri/src/services/model_pipeline.rs:375-385 — resolve_image_path_for_ml returns the ORIGINAL full-resolution path for all non-RAW images, ignoring the existing 800px thumbnail
src-tauri/src/db_core/embeddings.rs:301-309 — generate_embedding_for decodes the full image then resize_exact to 224x224 (input_size 224 at :63)
src-tauri/src/services/model_pipeline.rs:210-213 — engine mutex held across each inference
src-tauri/src/commands/embeddings.rs:279-298 — Tauri command runs the whole synchronous run inline in the async command (blocks a tokio worker and the invoke until done)
src-tauri/src/mcp/tools.rs:2083-2097 — MCP path spawns the same synchronous loop on tauri::async_runtime::spawn (not spawn_blocking)
Runtime observation: live library has 279/2,892 images embedded for clip-vit-b32 (sqlite read-only query against ~/Library/Application Support/com.glebkalinin.cull/cull.db)

Measured vs threshold: spec has no hard embedding-throughput number; complexity estimate at 10k images: full-res decode (4-50MP PNG) dominates at ~0.3-2s/image single-threaded = 1-5+ hours per full run
proposed_fix: Feed the 800px generated thumbnail (or 256px variant) to the embedding decode path for non-RAW images, wrap the run in spawn_blocking, batch the model_run_item + embedding inserts into one transaction per N images, and optionally parallelize decode with a small worker pool while keeping ONNX inference serialized.

title: Import + thumbnail pipeline is fully sequential on one core inside an async command (first 10k import takes hours)
evidence: src-tauri/src/commands/import.rs:62-85 — entire import loop (per file: sha256 + decode + thumbnails) runs inline in the async import_folder command
src-tauri/src/db_core/import.rs:106-143 — import_file decodes and generates thumbnails inline per file
src-tauri/src/db_core/thumbnails.rs:37-56 / 71-90 — four Lanczos3 resizes + four JPEG encodes per image (sizes 64/128/256/800)
src-tauri/src/services/import.rs:105-114 — same sequential loop on the MCP/service path; no transaction batching, each insert is its own WAL commit

Measured vs threshold: not a named threshold, but it gates the stranger's first-10-minutes experience: thumbnail pipeline inspection point. Observed thumbnail store: 11,502 files / 287MB for 2,892 images
Mitigating: progress events are emitted per file (commands/import.rs:68-75), so the UI shows progress
proposed_fix: Parallelize the decode+thumbnail stage across a rayon/thread pool (CPU-bound, embarrassingly parallel), batch DB inserts per chunk in a single transaction, and run the loop under spawn_blocking so the tokio runtime is not pinned.

title: Cold start: session restore refetches ALL previously loaded pages serially before the grid paints anything
evidence: src/lib/image-loading.ts:242-252 — do/while loop awaits pages of 200 sequentially and only calls images.set(loaded) after the loop completes

src/routes/+page.svelte:328-333 — onMount passes minItems = max(restored loadedImageCount, focusedIndex+1), so a previous deep-scroll session forces up to 50 serial IPC round-trips at 10k before first paint

Measured vs threshold (cold start < 3s): single page query measured at 29ms for 200 rows at 2,887 live images (sqlite .timer, read-only); Rust setup is lean (src-tauri/src/lib.rs:235-371 — no model loads, watcher non-scanning at notify-watch level). Fresh start passes comfortably; a restored 10k-deep session at ~30-60ms/page x 50 pages + IPC serialization risks breaching 3s

src/routes/+page.svelte:338-357 — check_library_health (2 stat() calls x N images, src-tauri/src/commands/library.rs:218-251) runs after loadImages so it does not gate first paint, but it does full-library file stats on every launch
proposed_fix: Paint after the first page: set images incrementally inside the restore loop (or set after page 1 and continue loading in the background), and clamp restored minItems to a small multiple of the viewport.

title: Loupe never loads the original full-resolution image — the 'loupe open < 1s full-res' threshold is unmeetable by design
evidence: src/lib/view-utils.ts:218-226 — chooseLoupeImagePath ignores isRaw/sourceLoadFailed and delegates to safeAssetPreviewPath

src/lib/view-utils.ts:200-216 — safeAssetPreviewPath returns thumbnail_path whenever it is asset-protocol-safe; the original path is only used if it lives under thumbnails/ or generated/ dirs (isAssetProtocolSafePath, :132-140), which user library folders never do
src-tauri/src/db_core/thumbnails.rs:8 — largest generated thumbnail is 800px, so loupe zoom (up to 20x, src/lib/components/Loupe.svelte:232-243) magnifies an 800px JPEG

Measured vs threshold: 'cached thumb < 300ms' passes trivially (local 800px JPEG via asset protocol); 'full-res < 1s' fails categorically — full-res is never requested. Not runtime-measured; code-traced
proposed_fix: Decide deliberately: either (a) extend the asset-protocol scope/`readFile` path so loupe progressively swaps thumbnail -> original (keeping the security boundary explicit), or (b) ship 800px-preview-only as a documented v1 behavior and rewrite the threshold. Option (a): show cached thumb instantly, load original async, swap on decode.

title: File-watcher images:changed triggers full reload + cache invalidation + focus reset on every debounce flush
evidence: src/routes/+page.svelte:377-379 — listen('images:changed') calls loadImages({force:true, invalidateCache:true}) with default resetFocus=true (src/lib/image-loading.ts:205, 255-258), resetting focus/scroll to 0 and dropping loaded pages back to 200

src-tauri/src/watcher.rs:148-196 — sync thread flushes every 500ms with a 1.5s debounce and emits images:changed whenever any file changed; during a bulk copy/generation into a watched folder this fires repeatedly

Impact at 10k: a user mid-triage at scroll depth N loses position and the app refetches from offset 0 on every flush; repeated invalidation also defeats the scope cache (src/lib/image-loading.ts:183-185)
proposed_fix: On watcher-driven reloads pass resetFocus:false and preserve scroll/loaded-count (refetch up to the current nextOffset), or apply incremental inserts for synced files instead of a full scope reload.

title: generate_similarity_groups is O(n²) brute-force cosine clustering in memory
evidence: src-tauri/src/services/ai.rs:67-104 — nested loop over all embeddings: up to n² cosine computations on 512-dim vectors plus per-pair HashSet lookups, single-threaded; entire embedding set held in memory
Complexity at 10k embeddings: ~100M cosine ops (~51 GFLOP) ≈ tens of seconds to minutes single-threaded; at the deferred 100k it becomes hours
Runs via async command (src-tauri/src/commands/embeddings.rs:396-411) without spawn_blocking, pinning a tokio worker for the duration
Not covered by a named 10k threshold (find-similar top-k is a separate O(n) path and passes — see PERF-07)
proposed_fix: Short term: spawn_blocking + parallelize the inner loop (rayon) and pre-normalize vectors so cosine becomes a dot product. Longer term: reuse the existing perceptual-hash banding or an ANN index for candidate pruning.

title: Measured: query/search thresholds PASS at 10k by measurement + extrapolation (record for synthesis)
evidence: Measured on the live cull.db (read-only, sqlite .timer; 2,887 images, 4,218 files, 18MB DB): list_images 200-row page 29ms (db.rs:1272-1288 query); deep page OFFSET 2600: 6ms; smart-collection 6-LEFT-JOIN rating filter (db.rs:3227-3297 shape): 1ms; 13-way LIKE search_text clause (smart_collections.rs:318-371 shape): 15ms; list_folders full-path aggregation (db.rs:1392-1422): 57ms
Smart-collection / NL query p95 < 500ms: PASS — worst measured 57ms; linear 3.5x extrapolation to 10k stays ≤ ~200ms. NL path is regex parse -> same SQL (db_core/nl_parser.rs), no added cost

CLIP find-similar < 2s: PASS — embeddings blob scan 3ms @ 279 vectors (db.rs:1844-1880 O(n) scan); extrapolated ~0.1-0.3s at 10k; live MCP find_similar call returned instantly; result hydration is batched via get_images_by_ids (src/lib/similarity.ts:21-25, db.rs:2844-2865 — no N+1)

Thumbnail load p95 < 200ms: expected PASS, not browser-measured — pre-generated 64/128/256/800 JPEG variants via asset protocol with DPR-aware variant pick (src/lib/view-utils.ts:164-191), custom virtualization caps DOM (src/lib/components/Grid.svelte:46-53), bounded 300-entry LRU decode-warm cache with unload-behind (src/lib/prefetch-cache.ts:25-70)

Resident memory < 1.5GB: expected PASS, not measured (webview RSS not attributable from ps) — DOM virtualized, prefetch bounded, scope cache capped at 5 scopes (src/lib/image-loading.ts:27, 152-159); images store does grow with scroll depth (~10k rows of metadata, est. tens of MB)

Caveats folded in: f.path LIKE with bound parameter cannot use image_files_path_idx (db.rs:1431-1443) — full scan per folder page, fine at 10k (~tens of ms) but the first thing to index-fix if libraries grow; raw_metadata is selected and shipped on every list row (db.rs:1275-1284) — NULL in the live library, but sidecar-heavy libraries would inflate IPC payload and frontend store
proposed_fix: No action required for 10k release bar. File post-launch: (1) drop raw_metadata from list-query payloads (fetch on demand in loupe), (2) make folder scoping index-friendly (case_sensitive_like or a stored parent_dir column), before revisiting the deferred 100k target.

title: Dead dependency: virtua is declared but never imported (custom virtualization is what actually ships)
evidence: package.json:53 — "virtua": "^0.49.1" in dependencies
grep across src/**/*.{ts,svelte}: zero imports of 'virtua'; the grid uses hand-rolled windowing (src/lib/components/Grid.svelte:46-53, src/lib/view-utils.ts:258-296 computeVisibleItems)
proposed_fix: Remove virtua from package.json (and lockfile) — it misleads contributors about the virtualization mechanism and adds supply-chain surface for nothing.

title: Embedding Explorer transfers up to 5,000 x 512 float vectors as one JSON IPC payload
evidence: src/lib/components/EmbeddingExplorer.svelte:74,1234 — PROJECTION_EMBEDDING_LIMIT = 5000, fetched in a single getEmbeddingPage call

src-tauri/src/db_core/db.rs:1779-1827 — get_embedding_page decodes blobs to f32 and returns a flat vector; 5000 x 512 floats ≈ 2.56M numbers ≈ 25-40MB of JSON through Tauri IPC (seconds of serialize/parse, transient memory spike)
Mitigating: capped at 5000, projection runs in a worker (src/lib/embedding-projection.worker.ts), thumbnail cache bounded at 600 (EmbeddingExplorer.svelte:167)
proposed_fix: Page the vector fetch in chunks (e.g. 500) feeding the worker incrementally, or return base64-packed Float32 buffers instead of JSON number arrays.
